heading: ## Risk Matrix
body:
| Risk ID | Risk Description | Probability | Impact (1-5) | Mitigation Strategy |
|---------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|---------------------|
| R1 | Uncertainty in τ₂ measurement (stress-dependent erosion timescale) | Medium | 4 | Conduct OP-T1 experiment (long-term fibroblast culture under controlled oxidative stress); use single-cell Q-FISH to estimate τ₂ from slope of L_avg(t) |
| R2 | Inability to separate α₂ (division-dependent) and β₂ (time-dependent) erosion in vivo | High | 5 | Perform OP-T2 experiment (longitudinal telomere measurement in non-dividing cells vs. dividing cells); use linear mixed model with random intercept to partition variance |
| R3 | Neglect of telomerase activity in stem cells (confounds D₂ measurement) | Medium | 4 | Include telomerase-positive control cell lines; measure telomerase activity (TRAP assay) in all tissue samples; model telomerase as a separate counter or correction term |
| R4 | qPCR artifacts in telomere length measurement (e.g., DNA quality, primer bias) | Low | 3 | Validate all qPCR results with Q-FISH or Southern blot for a subset of samples; use standardized DNA extraction protocol; include internal control primers |
| R5 | Unaccounted heterogeneity in telomere length distribution within cell populations | High | 4 | Use single-cell Q-FISH or single-molecule telomere length assay; model distribution (not just mean) as in OP-T1; apply stochastic jump process if variance nonlinear |
| R6 | Oxidative stress effects confounded by other stressors (e.g., inflammation, UV) | Medium | 3 | Use controlled in vitro systems (e.g., paraquat dose-response); in vivo, measure multiple stress markers (8-oxo-dG, IL-6, CRP) and include as covariates |

heading: ## Consortium / Collaboration Plan
body:
**Planned collaborations (placeholders):**
- **Telomere biology laboratory** (e.g., group from PMID:24374808) – measurement of α₂ (division-dependent erosion rate) via long-term culture and Q-FISH.
- **Oxidative stress group** (e.g., group from PMID:39837827) – quantification of β₂ (stress-dependent erosion) under controlled ROS conditions.
- **Clinical cohort provider** (e.g., longitudinal aging study with telomere data) – in vivo validation of D₂ model predictions.
- **Bioinformatics core** – development of linear mixed models for α₂/β₂ separation (OP-T2) and stochastic jump process models (OP-T1).
- **EIC Pathfinder consortium** – integration of Telomere Counter #2 into MCAOA framework; details in WP1 of EIC Pathfinder proposal.

**Competencies required:**
- Single-cell Q-FISH and high-throughput telomere length measurement
- Cell culture under controlled oxygen and stress conditions
- Longitudinal clinical data collection and biobanking
- Statistical modeling (mixed effects, survival analysis, Bayesian inference)
- MCAOA integration and multi-counter coupling (Γ matrix estimation)

### Statistical Test Specification for Each Falsification Condition

**FalsCond1 (α₂ ≤ 10 bp/PD):**
- Null hypothesis H₀: α₂ ≤ 10 bp/PD (one-sided test)
- Alternative hypothesis H₁: α₂ > 10 bp/PD
- Statistical test: one-sample t-test (or one-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank if normality violated)
- Significance level: α = 0.05
- Power: 0.80 (as per power analysis: N ≥ 10 independent cell lines)
- Effect size: expected α₂ = 5 bp/PD (from PMID:24374808), SD = 4 bp/PD

**FalsCond2 (β₂ > 0 bp/year):**
- Null hypothesis H₀: β₂ ≤ 0 bp/year (no stress-dependent erosion)
- Alternative hypothesis H₁: β₂ > 0 bp/year
- Statistical test: linear mixed model with random intercept for each cell line; test fixed effect of time (slope β₂)
- Significance level: α = 0.05
- Power: 0.80 (as per power analysis: N ≥ 12 independent cell lines per condition)
- Effect size: expected slope difference of 5 bp/PD between stress and control conditions, SD = 4 bp/PD

**FalsCond3 (n₂* ≤ 50 PDs):**
- Null hypothesis H₀: n₂* ≥ 50 PDs (Hayflick limit not reached earlier)
- Alternative hypothesis H₁: n₂* < 50 PDs
- Statistical test: Kaplan-Meier survival analysis (event = senescence onset) with log-rank test
- Significance level: α = 0.05
- Power: 0.80 (N ≥ 10 cell lines per condition)

**FalsCond4 (τ₂ finite and measurable):**
- Null hypothesis H₀: τ₂ → ∞ (no time-dependent erosion)
- Alternative hypothesis H₁: τ₂ < ∞ (finite stress-dependent erosion timescale)
- Statistical test: likelihood ratio test comparing model with τ₂ vs. model without τ₂ (nested models)
- Significance level: α = 0.05
- Power: 0.80 (N ≥ 12 cell lines per condition, as per OP-T1 power analysis)

line_anchor: ## OP-T1: Количественное определение константы времени стресс-зависимой эрозии (`τ₂`)
body:
### Risk Assessment for OP-T1

| Outcome | Probability | Impact on Model | Action (Model Update Protocol) |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|--------------------------------|
| 1. L_avg(t) linear, Var(t) linear | 0.40 (moderate) | Low – confirms current parameterization | Estimate τ₂ = β₂ / slope; update PARAMETERS.md |
| 2. L_avg(t) nonlinear, Var(t) jump | 0.30 (moderate) | High – requires reformulation | Replace β₂·(t/τ₂) with stochastic jump process; update CONCEPT.md §2 |
| 3. L_avg(t) unchanged, Var(t) jump, TIF+ | 0.20 (low) | Critical – paradigm shift | Switch to subpopulation model; update CONCEPT.md §1 and §2 |
| 4. No significant change in L_avg or Var | 0.10 (low) | Critical – falsification | Reject β₂ contribution in vitro; revise CONCEPT.md §2; design in vivo test |

**Decision rule:** If outcome 2, 3, or 4 occurs, initiate Model Update Protocol (see DESIGN.md §5).

line_anchor: ## OP-T2: Экспериментальное разделение вкладов `α₂` и `β₂` in vivo
body:
### Risk Assessment for OP-T2

| Outcome | Probability | Impact on Model | Action (Model Update Protocol) |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|--------------------------------|
| 1. α₂ and β₂ separable (distinct slopes) | 0.50 (moderate) | Low – confirms model | Update PARAMETERS.md with in vivo estimates |
| 2. α₂ and β₂ confounded (collinear) | 0.30 (moderate) | High – requires additional experiments | Design intervention study (e.g., antioxidant treatment) to break collinearity |
| 3. β₂ not detectable in vivo | 0.15 (low) | High – revise model | Remove β₂ term for in vivo predictions; update CONCEPT.md §2 |
| 4. α₂ not detectable in vivo | 0.05 (low) | Critical – paradigm shift | Reconsider role of division-dependent erosion in vivo; update CONCEPT.md §1 |

**Decision rule:** If outcome 2, 3, or 4 occurs, initiate Model Update Protocol (see DESIGN.md §5).

heading: ## Suggested Measurement Protocols for Uncertain Parameters
body:
### Parameters marked as "To Be Calibrated" or "Hypothesized"

**α₂ (division-dependent erosion rate):**
- Protocol: Long-term culture of primary human fibroblasts (e.g., IMR-90 or BJ) at 5% O₂, 5% CO₂, 37°C. Passage every 3-4 days. Measure telomere length every 5 PDs via Q-FISH (≥100 cells per time point). Fit linear regression of mean telomere length vs. PD. Expected value: 5 bp/PD (PMID:24374808).
- Reference: OP-T1 (OPEN_PROBLEMS.md) for detailed design.

**β₂ (stress-dependent erosion rate):**
- Protocol: Same as α₂, but with controlled oxidative stress (e.g., 50 µM paraquat or 20% O₂). Measure telomere length every 3-5 days. Compare slope to control (α₂ alone). β₂ = slope_stress - slope_control.
- Reference: OP-T1 (OPEN_PROBLEMS.md) for detailed design.

**τ₂ (stress-dependent erosion timescale):**
- Protocol: From same experiment as β₂, estimate τ₂ as time for mean telomere length to decrease by β₂ bp (i.e., τ₂ = β₂ / slope_stress). Alternatively, fit nonlinear model D₂(t) = α₂·n(t) + β₂·(t/τ₂) using maximum likelihood.
- Reference: OP-T1 (OPEN_PROBLEMS.md) for detailed design.

**n₂* (Hayflick limit):**
- Protocol: Culture cells until senescence (population doubling time > 1 week, >90% SA-β-gal positive). Record PD at senescence. Use Kaplan-Meier survival analysis across multiple cell lines.
- Reference: Standard senescence assay protocols.

**Γ₂,ᵢ (coupling coefficients with other counters):**
- Protocol: For each counter i (e.g., MitoROS #3), measure both D₂ and Dᵢ in same cells under perturbation (e.g., mitochondrial stress). Compute correlation coefficient or fit linear model D₂ = Γ₂,ᵢ·Dᵢ + ε.
- Reference: DESIGN.md §4 for coupling matrix estimation.
